sample_errors.question: What is the unabbreviated mailing street address of the school with the highest FRPM count for K-12
sample_errors.pred_sql: SELECT T1.MailStreet FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.`School Name` = T2.School ORDER BY T1.`FRPM Count (K-12)` DESC LIMIT 1
sample_errors.gold_sql: SELECT T2.MailStreet FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY T1.`FRPM Count (K-12)` DESC LIMIT 1

sample_errors.question: For the school with the highest average score in Reading in the SAT test, what is its FRPM count for
sample_errors.pred_sql: SELECT T1.FrpmCount FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds ORDER BY T2.AvgScrRead DESC LIMIT 1
sample_errors.gold_sql: SELECT T2.`FRPM Count (Ages 5-17)` FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY T1.AvgScrRead DESC LIMIT 1

sample_errors.question: List the top five schools, by descending order, from the highest to the lowest, the most number of E
sample_errors.pred_sql: SELECT T1.NCESSchool, T1.Enroll12 FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds ORDER BY T1.Enroll12 DESC LIMIT 5
sample_errors.gold_sql: SELECT T1.NCESSchool FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` DESC LIMIT 5

sample_errors.question: How many schools in merged Alameda have number of test takers less than 100?
sample_errors.pred_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.cds) FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T1.County = 'Alameda' AND T2.NumTstTakr < 100
sample_errors.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.CDSCode) FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T1.StatusType = 'Merged' AND T2.NumTstTakr < 100 AND T1.County = 'Lake'

sample_errors.question: How many schools in Fresno (directly funded) have number of test takers not more than 250?
sample_errors.pred_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.cds) FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T1.FundingType = 'Direct' AND T1.City = 'Fresno' AND T2.NumTstTakr <= 250
sample_errors.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.CDSCode) FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T1.`Charter Funding Type` = 'Directly funded' AND T1.`County Name` = 'Fresno' AND T2.NumTstTakr <= 250

sample_errors.question: Give the names of the schools with the percent eligible for free meals in K-12 is more than 0.1 and
sample_errors.pred_sql: SELECT T1.School FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T2.NumGE1500 >= 1500 AND T1.`Percent (%) Eligible Free (K-12)` > 0.1
sample_errors.gold_sql: SELECT T2.`School Name` FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE CAST(T2.`Free Meal Count (K-12)` AS REAL) / T2.`Enrollment (K-12)` > 0.1 AND T1.NumGE1500 > 0
sample_errors.error: no such column: T1.Percent (%) Eligible Free (K-12)

sample_errors.question: What is the average score in writing for the schools that were opened after 1991 or closed before 20
sample_errors.pred_sql: SELECT T1.AvgScrWrite, T1.School, T1.Phone FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.OpenDate > '1991-01-01' OR T2.ClosedDate < '2000-01-01'
sample_errors.gold_sql: SELECT T2.School, T1.AvgScrWrite, T2.Phone FROM schools AS T2 LEFT JOIN satscores AS T1 ON T2.CDSCode = T1.cds WHERE strftime('%Y', T2.OpenDate) > '1991' OR strftime('%Y', T2.ClosedDate) < '2000'

sample_errors.question: Consider the average difference between K-12 enrollment and 15-17 enrollment of schools that are loc
sample_errors.pred_sql: SELECT T1.School, T2.DOCType FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.`School Code` WHERE T1.FundingType = 'Local' ORDER BY T2.`Enrollment (K-12)` - T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` DESC
sample_errors.gold_sql: SELECT T2.School, T2.DOC FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.FundingType = 'Locally funded' AND (T1.`Enrollment (K-12)` - T1.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) > (SELECT AVG(T3.`Enrollment (K-12)` - T3.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) FROM frpm AS T3 INNER JOIN schools AS T4

sample_errors.question: Which cities have the top 5 lowest enrollment number for students in grades 1 through 12?
sample_errors.pred_sql: SELECT T2.City FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.`School Code` ORDER BY T2.`Enrollment (K-12)` ASC LIMIT 5
sample_errors.gold_sql: SELECT T2.City FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode GROUP BY T2.City ORDER BY SUM(T1.`Enrollment (K-12)`) ASC LIMIT 5

sample_errors.question: What is the eligible free rate of the 10th and 11th schools with the highest enrolment for students
sample_errors.pred_sql: SELECT T1.`Percent (%) Eligible Free (K-12)` FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY T2.enroll12 DESC LIMIT 10, 11
sample_errors.gold_sql: SELECT CAST(`Free Meal Count (K-12)` AS REAL) / `Enrollment (K-12)` FROM frpm ORDER BY `Enrollment (K-12)` DESC LIMIT 9, 2

sample_successes.question: What is the highest eligible free rate for K-12 students in the schools in Alameda County?
sample_successes.pred_sql:
SELECT MAX(T1.`Percent (%) Eligible Free (K-12)`) 
FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.County = 'Alameda'

sample_successes.question: How many schools with an average score in Math greater than 400 in the SAT test are exclusively virt
sample_successes.pred_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.CDSCode) FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T2.AvgScrMath > 400 AND T1.Virtual = 'F'

sample_successes.question: Among the schools with the SAT test takers of over 500, please list the schools that are magnet scho
sample_successes.pred_sql: SELECT T1.School FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T2.NumTstTakr > 500 AND (T1.Magnet = 1 OR T1.GSoffered = 'Magnet')

sample_successes.question: What is the phone number of the school that has the highest number of test takers with an SAT score
sample_successes.pred_sql: SELECT T1.Phone FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T2.NumGE1500 > 0 ORDER BY T2.NumGE1500 DESC LIMIT 1

sample_successes.question: What is the number of SAT test takers of the schools with the highest FRPM count for K-12 students?
sample_successes.pred_sql: SELECT T2.NumTstTakr FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.`School Name` = T2.sname ORDER BY T1.`FRPM Count (K-12)` DESC LIMIT 1
